page: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-8g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance within the Cloud GPU family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by 2 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and a 50 GB local NVMe SSD. For hardware acceleration, it includes a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU representing 0.125 of a physical GPU, which provides 2 GB of dedicated VRAM.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile. The shared Intel CPU delivers average single-core performance in stress-ng and gzip compression, but falls into the lower tiers for multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations. However, the presence of the NVIDIA Ampere GPU enables top-tier prompt processing speeds for small LLM models (135M) at 7078.28 tokens/sec, alongside average performance for text generation on small models and prompt processing on medium (7B) models.

This instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads by utilizing fractional GPU and shared CPU resources. It avoids the high cost of dedicated full-GPU instances while providing hardware acceleration. The server is suited for lightweight LLM inference, GPU-accelerated application development, testing, and basic media processing workloads that do not require massive CPU or memory bandwidth.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-8g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance designed for entry-level accelerated workloads. It features 2 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs at 2.0 GHz, 8.0 GB of system memory, and a 50 GB NVMe SSD. Acceleration is provided by a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 2 GB of VRAM. While CPU-bound benchmarks and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, the GPU enables top-tier prompt processing speeds of 7078.28 tokens/sec for small LLM models. This configuration offers a cost-effective option for developers needing GPU access without the expense of a dedicated card. It is best suited for lightweight LLM inference, GPU application testing, and basic media processing.

tagline: A cost-efficient fractional GPU instance combining shared Intel processors with NVIDIA Ampere acceleration for lightweight AI inference and development.
bullet_points: Fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 2 GB VRAM
2 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory
50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier prompt processing performance for small LLM models
Suited for lightweight LLM inference and GPU application development
